records.ety: [L. synecdoche, Gr. synekdochh`, fr. to receive jointly; sy`n with + <?/ to receive; <?/ out + <?/ to receive.]

defs: A figure or trope by which a part of a thing is put for the whole (as, fifty sail for fifty ships), or the whole for a part (as, the smiling year for spring), the species for the genus (as, cutthroat for assassin), the genus for the species (as, a creature for a man), the name of the material for the thing made, etc.
